VIVIANA POWER TECH: COMMISSIONS 400 KV TRANSMISSION LINE FOR 323.4 MW WIND PARK ⚡🌬️

• ⚡ Viviana Power Tech has successfully commissioned a 400 kV Double-Circuit LILO transmission line for a 323.4 MW wind park in Kachchh, Gujarat, with the line being energised on July 10, 2026. 

• 🏗️ The project involved executing three high-voltage crossings, including a 765 kV under-crossing, ±500 kV HVDC under-crossing and a 400 kV overhead crossing, along with live-line stringing without a grid shutdown. 

• 📊 As of 21 July 2026, the company has a confirmed order book of ~₹1,340 crore (including BESS), bids under evaluation worth ~₹936 crore, and identified tenders exceeding ₹2,500 crore. 

• 🌱 The commissioned transmission line enables evacuation of renewable power from the wind park to the high-voltage grid, strengthening India's clean energy transmission infrastructure. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful commissioning showcases Viviana's execution capabilities, strengthens its credentials in EHV transmission projects and enhances long-term growth visibility backed by a strong order pipeline.)

MACPOWER CNC: ACQUIRES 13 ACRES ON 30-YEAR LEASE FOR ₹50 CRORE EXPANSION 🏭🚀

• 🏗️ Macpower CNC Machines has acquired legally registered leasehold rights over ~13 acres of land (including 4 acres of green zone) near Metoda GIDC, Rajkot, on a 30-year lease basis. 

• 💰 The company plans to invest around ₹50 crore to develop manufacturing infrastructure, plant & machinery and utilities, with the project targeted for completion within 12 months. 

• ⚙️ The new facility will support debottlenecking of existing operations, backward integration, component manufacturing and future capacity expansion, building on the company's existing 2,500 machines per annum capacity. 

• 🎯 The project is eligible for benefits under the Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026, including up to 25% capital subsidy on plant & machinery/buildings and 7% interest subsidy. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic land acquisition and planned capacity expansion strengthen Macpower's long-term manufacturing capabilities, improve growth visibility and support future scale-up.)

GOVERNMENT SHORTLISTS 230 PRODUCTS ACROSS SECTORS FOR INDIGENISATION - ETNOW

A total of 230 products have been placed in the priority basket after analysing 335 products

The products have been shortlisted to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence amid global uncertainties

Categorised into three segments: Quicker Indigenisation, Scalable Indigenisation, and Aspirational Indigenisation

The indigenisation of prioritised products will be pursued through short-, medium-, and long-term plans

Working groups reviewed products across 25 ministries and departments

Alert: Government had constituted six working groups to identify products for indigenisation

Products from sectors such as medical devices, chemicals, textiles, automotive and EVs, capital goods, renewable energy, power, semiconductors, defence, electronics, space, and telecommunications, among others, have been prioritised

The working groups also identified key barriers to indigenisation

Support is likely to be provided in the form of PLI, demand guarantees, and subsidies based on the strategic plan

BRIGADE ENTERPRISES: LAUNCHES ₹300+ CRORE RESIDENTIAL PROJECT IN MYSURU 🏡🚀

• 🏗️ Brigade Enterprises has launched "Brigade Misty Greens", a premium residential project in Mysuru through a Joint Development Agreement (JDA), with a revenue potential of over ₹300 crore. 

• 📍 The 4.5-acre project features 3 & 4 BHK apartments along with 14 exclusive duplex units, targeting premium homebuyers and long-term investors.

reports.text:
NHPC: 10 DEAD AFTER TUNNEL ACCIDENT AT 500 MW TEESTA-VI PROJECT ⚠️🏗️

• 🚨 An explosion caused by suspected methane gas trapped inside rocks occurred in the Head Race Tunnel (HRT) of the under-construction 500 MW Teesta Stage-VI Hydroelectric Project in Sikkim on 20 July 2026. 

• 🚑 NHPC immediately activated emergency response measures and is coordinating with NDRF, SDRF, district administration, DG Mines Safety and other agencies for rescue operations. 

• 👷 According to the latest update, 25 personnel were trapped, of which 10 fatalities have been reported so far, while evacuation and rescue efforts continue. 

• 🔍 NHPC said a detailed investigation will be conducted to determine the cause of the incident and the company is extending full support to the rescue agencies and affected families. 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The tragic accident may lead to project delays, regulatory scrutiny and increased focus on safety measures at the Teesta-VI project.)

*MSCI Aug'26 Review: Initial Cut-off Assessment after Day 1 | Biocon at a crucial juncture*

_Nuvama Alternative & Quant Research_

• The MSCI August review cut-off window commenced yesterday, and we are reiterating our assessment based on Day 1 of the cut-off period.

• Historically, MSCI has tended to determine the cut-off within the initial few days of the 10-working-day observation window.

• Our current view remains unchanged >>
* Three high-conviction inclusions
* Three high-conviction exclusions

• Biocon continues to be the only stock on the borderline. Based purely on Day 1 price cut-off, the stock missed the inclusion threshold by approximately 2.4%.

• If Biocon trades comfortably above ₹447 today and Day 2 is eventually chosen by MSCI as the cut-off date, the stock has the potential to qualify for inclusion.

• The actual cut-off date used by MSCI will only be known on August 13, when the review announcement is released.

*Key Dates*
* Cut-off Window: July 20 – July 31
* Announcement: August 13 (3:30 AM IST)
* Effective Date: August 31

• 🛣️ The project involves Independent Engineering Services for the construction supervision of the 4-lane Patna–Arrah–Sasaram Corridor (Package-2) under the Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM) in Bihar.

• ⏳ The contract has a 48-month execution period, comprising 6 months development, 24 months construction and 18 months O&M, and is awarded by a domestic entity. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The NHAI order strengthens Marc Technocrats' order book, enhances its presence in highway engineering consultancy and supports future revenue visibility.)

reports.text:
GLENMARK: US FDA APPROVES EXPANDED USE OF RYALTRIS FOR CHILDREN 👧👦🇺🇸

• 🇺🇸 US FDA has approved RYALTRIS® Nasal Spray for children aged 6 to below 12 years with Seasonal Allergic Rhinitis (SAR)

• 📈 Approval expands RYALTRIS' eligible patient population from 12+ years to 6+ years, broadening its addressable market

• 🧪 Approval is backed by a Phase 3 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study involving 446 children

• 💊 Glenmark assumed direct commercialization and distribution of RYALTRIS in the US from April 1, 2026, strengthening control over brand strategy and market execution

• 🌍 RYALTRIS is now approved across multiple global markets and was launched in 11 additional countries in FY26, taking its presence to 56 countries

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expands the addressable patient base in the US, strengthens Glenmark's respiratory portfolio, and supports long-term growth of the RYALTRIS franchise.)

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Rural conditions have weakened but remain stable; further monsoon progress will be the key determinant for demand trends.)

reports.text:
CLSA on Paytm ; U-P, TP Rs 1050

1QFY27 Ebitda of Rs2bn was marginally ahead of our Rs1.9bn estimate.

Acceleration in payments GMV growth to 31% YoY from mid-20s in preceding quarter stood out
Financial services (FS) revenue growth also accelerated from a high-30s percentage to a mid-40s percentage in quarter

However, calculated net take rate in payments moderated from 8.8bps to 8.3bps sequentially.
With stable contribution margin at 55%, lower fixed costs growth led to Ebitda improving from Rs1.3bn to Rs2bn sequentially.

Opex also benefitted from lower provision on doubtful debt this quarter.

Its subsidiary, Paytm Payment Services, has applied for a wallet license

Trim FY27-29 Ebitda estimates by 2-3% due to higher opex.
Recent run-up in stock price in anticipation of MDR on UPI returning leaves no upside, even if it happens

SUTLEJ TEXTILES: Senior management executive Jitender Kumar resigns effective July 17, 2026.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

📍 ADDITIONS

📍 OTHERS

FERMENTA BIOTECH: ENVIRONMENT SOLUTIONS ARM TO CEASE AS WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARY 🤝

• 🏢 Fermenta Environmental Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (FESPL) has entered into a share subscription agreement with Aasava Management Services LLP

• 📉 After allotment of 7,17,949 equity shares to Aasava, Fermenta's stake in FESPL will reduce to 91.76%

• 🔄 FESPL will cease to be a wholly-owned subsidiary, while remaining a subsidiary of the company

• 📅 Share allotment is expected to be completed on or before 27 July 2026

• 🌱 Company said Aasava's investment supports FESPL's long-term growth and is expected to be value accretive for the business

🟢 Impact: Positive (Brings in a strategic investor while Fermenta retains majority control, supporting the subsidiary's future growth.)

EMCURE: CDSCO APPROVES POVITZRA FOR MASH TREATMENT 💊

• 🇮🇳 CDSCO has approved Povitzra (oral semaglutide) for treating adults with non-cirrhotic MASH with moderate to advanced liver fibrosis (F2-F3)

• 🧬 Povitzra contains Novo Nordisk's oral semaglutide, expanding Emcure's gastroenterology and hepatology portfolio through subsidiary Zuventus Healthcare

• 🩺 Approval addresses a significant unmet need in MASH, a progressive liver disease with limited treatment options

• 📈 Strengthens Emcure's innovative metabolic disease portfolio and expands access to evidence-based therapy in India

🟢 Impact: Positive (New indication approval broadens Emcure's product portfolio and creates additional growth opportunities in a large unmet therapy segment.)

Dynamic Cables | Q1 FY27 Highlights

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹349.1 crore, +33% YoY.
- Operating Profit ₹38.0 crore, +41% YoY.
- PAT ₹25.0 crore, +37% YoY.
- Operating Margin 10.9% vs 10.3%.

Business Performance
- Growth led by renewables.
- Strong private utility demand.
- International business expanded.
- US market entry supported exports.

Order Book & Expansion
- Order book ₹811 crore.
- Strong revenue visibility.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Long-term demand remains healthy.

Balance Sheet
- Receivable days around 69.
- Borrowings reduced to ₹135.8 crore.
- Cash balance ₹47.0 crore.
- Net debt ₹88.8 crore.

Management Outlook
- Demand remains robust.
- Better product mix supporting margins.
- Infrastructure spending remains tailwind.
- International expansion to drive growth.

Key Takeaway
- Dynamic Cables delivered a strong Q1FY27 with healthy revenue growth, margin expansion and improved profitability. A robust ₹811 crore order book, expanding exports and ongoing capacity expansion provide strong visibility for sustained long-term growth.

reports.text:
Reverse-engineering the small-cap smart money

We pulled the latest portfolios of 6 top small-cap funds (JM, TrustMF, Helios, Bank of India, Motilal Oswal, Abakkus) and ran an overlap screen. What the "best-placed" managers actually agree on:

1. Only 1 stock is held by 3+ of them → Acutaas Chemicals (Ami Organics). Navin Fluorine & Cemindia show up in 2 each.

2. Pharma/CDMO is the only sector all 6 are overweight. Then power/electricals capex (BoI at 12.7%!) and auto ancillaries.

3. The real edge isn't the consensus — it's the high-conviction singles: Garware Hi-Tech (JM 5.6%), Va Tech Wabag (Motilal), MTAR (Helios). One manager, one big call, nobody else in.

But consensus ≠ conviction to buy. Small-caps trade ~33× vs ~28× large-cap, and half these funds are <1yr old. This is a starting universe and one of the multiple ways to screen from — not a buy list.

Disc: Educational research only. Not advice

Financial Performance

India Cements Integration
- PAT improved to ₹52 crore.

- Volumes up 18.5%.
- Integration progressing successfully.
- Asset profitability improving.

Sustainability Initiatives
- WHRS capacity reached 434 MW.

- Green power mix 47%.
- Focus on energy efficiency.
- Lower carbon footprint.

Capacity Expansion
- Global capacity target 242.5 MTPA.
- FY27 addition 15.9 MTPA.
- FY28 addition 29.8 MTPA.
- Expansion across multiple states.
- Growth pipeline remains strong.

Strategic Developments
- Entering wires and cables.
- Business launch preparations underway.
- Diversifying building materials portfolio.
- Vivek Agrawal re-appointed as WTD & CMO.
- Leadership continuity maintained.

Key Takeaway
- UltraTech Cement delivered a better-than-expected Q1FY27 with strong volume growth, healthy profitability and successful integration of India Cements. Aggressive capacity expansion, increasing green energy usage and diversification into the wires & cables business reinforce its long-term leadership in the building materials sector.

ULTRATECH - Solid set of earnings

IRDAI views commission rates earned by lenders marketing credit life insurance as steep

Alert: Credit life insurance is a policy linked to a loan, and repays outstanding debt if borrower passes away

Alert: Lenders market credit protection on loans to cover possible defaults on borrower's death

IRDAI would likely unveil consultation paper on commission distribution rules by July end

New policy may become effective from 2027

Lower commission on credit protection policies may check the pace of mis-selling insurance

Baazar Style has disclosed two civil proceedings against Cupid ltd / related parties

This is interesting because Cupid had recently come in as a strategic investor in Baazar Style through preferential warrants

Cupid subscribed to 1.01 Cr equity warrants at 328.25 per warrant, aggregating to 331.53 Cr. Baazar Style had received 25% upfront amount of 82.88 Cr from Cupid 

And now within a few months, Baazar Style has filed two civil suits against Cupid

The company has claimed a decree of 28.18 Cr along with other consequential reliefs

So basically, Cupid first invests via preferential allotment, and now Baazar Style is taking legal action against Cupid / related parties

What the heck is happening here?

When a strategic investor enters through a large preferential issue & soon after becomes part of litigation with the company, it raises a lot of questions

They also stopped doing concalls which is a big red flag

Govt Eases Battery PLI Norms to Attract More Storage Players — Final 10 GWh Tender Under ₹18,100 Crore ACC Scheme Floated

The New Tender — Key Details
Government floated new 10 GWh tender for grid-scale energy storage — final part of larger 50 GWh initiative

Under ₹18,100 crore Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC)

Floated on 15 July by Ministry of Heavy Industries (MHI)

Tender will close on 13 October; bids to be opened on 14 October

Pre-bid conference to be held on 29 July

Key Changes from 2021 Version — Eased Norms
Easier entry requirements

Shift from energy density to battery efficiency as key metric

Lower subsidies per kilowatt-hour (kWh) — reflecting falling costs of cell manufacturing globally

Minimum and maximum bid capacity thresholds revised to 1 GWh and 4 GWh respectively — to encourage wider participation and attract more bidders for remaining 10 GWh capacity

Revised limits retain same 1:4 minimum-to-maximum bid capacity ratio as earlier 5–20 GWh bidding framework — ensuring consistency with original scheme design

History of the Scheme — Previous Awards
In 2021 (first bidding round): government awarded 50 GWh capacity earmarked for grid-scale storage

MHI called for bids on 15 July — almost two years after the last award under the scheme

Four bidders in previous round:
Ola Electric — 20 GWh
Reliance Industries Ltd — 5 GWh
Rajesh Exports — 5 GWh
Hyundai Global Motors — 20 GWh

After clarifications from Hyundai Motor that 'Hyundai Global Motors' was not associated with the Korean manufacturer — government rescinded the award

Of the 20 GWh capacity rescinded — 10 GWh was awarded to Reliance New Energy (subsidiary of Reliance Industries) in September 2024

Last remaining 10 GWh is what is now being tendered

Per-kWh Subsidy — Reduced
MNRE confirmed per-kWh subsidy has been reduced in this tender

Reflecting falling costs of cell manufacturing globally

Core Theme 
The government's revised ACC PLI tender for the final 10 GWh reflects hard-won lessons from the 2021 round — by lowering entry barriers, shifting to efficiency metrics, reducing per-kWh subsidies in line with global cost trends and revising bid capacity thresholds to attract more participants, the MHI is prioritising broader competition and successful execution over headline capacity numbers, even as India's grid-scale storage ambitions remain critical to managing its fast-growing renewable energy penetration.

- DSIR-recognised R&D centre.
- Focus on automotive and sustainable materials.

Capacity Expansion
- Capacity increasing to 100,000 MTPA.
- Current capacity 75,000 MTPA.
- Capex around ₹200 crore.
- Fully funded through internal accruals.
- Commissioning targeted by September 2026.
- Full utilisation expected by FY28-end.

Key Takeaway
- Bhansali Engineering continues to strengthen its competitive moat through technology partnerships, backward integration, disciplined capital allocation and sustained R&D. The upcoming capacity expansion, funded entirely through internal cash flows, positions the company for long-term volume growth while maintaining a strong balance sheet.

AI Data Centres | Sector Update

Structural Growth
- Emerging as a key real estate vertical.
- Long-term, sticky rental income.
- ₹1.6–2 trillion investment opportunity.

Industry Outlook
- IT load to rise from 1.6 GW to 14–15 GW by FY35.
- Most capacity still under development.
- Major earnings expected over 3–5 years.

Key Challenges
- Power and transmission availability.
- Fibre, water and regulatory approvals.
- Hyperscaler partnerships remain critical.

Key Beneficiaries
- Anant Raj.
- Mindspace REIT.
- DLF.
- Macrotech Developers (Lodha).
- Adani Enterprises (AdaniConneX).

Key Takeaway
- AI data centres represent a multi-year structural growth opportunity, with developers having strong infrastructure access and hyperscaler partnerships best positioned to benefit.

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

- **Craftsman Automation:** **Buy**, TP **₹11,600**.
- **Samvardhana Motherson:** **Neutral**, TP **₹148**.

- Margins missed expectations.

- Services grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.

**Growth Outlook**

- EBITDA missed estimates.

**Kotak Mahindra Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**

- Broad-based loan growth.
- Asset quality remained stable.
- Loan growth matched peers.

**Financial Highlights**
- NIM declined sequentially.
- LDR improved.
- RoA remained above **2%**.

**Key Monitorables**

- Capital levels increased.
- RoE remained constrained.

**Axis Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

- Corporate-led loan growth.
- Balance sheet remained strong.

- Up **23% YoY**.
- Beat estimates.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

- Loan growth **19% YoY**.
- NIM down **16 bps QoQ**.
- Corporate mix impacted margins.

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality remained strong.
- Credit cost **0.7%**.
- Retail deposits improving.
- Fee income recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,700**.
- FY27 EPS raised **2%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Axis Bank, supported by strong loan growth, healthy asset quality and improving retail deposits, with margin pressure expected to ease in 2HFY27.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹19,100 crore**.
- Up **5% YoY**.
- Results in line.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth **16% YoY**.
- Corporate lending increased.
- NIM down **12 bps QoQ**.
- NII growth **7%**.

**Profitability**
- Opex growth moderated.
- Credit costs declined.
- Low slippages supported profits.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,050**.
- PBT CAGR seen at **15%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains constructive on HDFC Bank, with strong loan growth, disciplined costs and lower credit costs supporting earnings despite continued margin compression from higher corporate lending.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Results in line.
- Margins declined QoQ.
- PAT met estimates.
- Credit costs stable.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth accelerated.
- Deposit growth improved.
- Asset quality remained pristine.
- Growth momentum strengthened.

**Outlook**

**Jyoti CNC | Investment Snapshot**

**Brokerage View**
- YES Securities TP **₹1,075**.
- Sunidhi TP **₹1,250**.
- Nirmal Bang TP **₹976**.
- Upside potential **34–80%**.
- All maintain **Buy** ratings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Record order book **₹4,732 crore**.
- Revenue visibility **18–24 months**.
- Orders up **30%+ YoY**.
- Engineering orders up **58%**.
- Auto orders up **23%**.
- Defence orders up **23%**.

**Capacity Expansion**
- Capacity tripling underway.
- Current **6,000 machines/year**.
- Target **16,000 machines/year**.
- Expansion by **Sep-2026**.
- Plants operating above capacity.

**Financial Strength**
- FY26 revenue **₹1,949 crore**.
- Revenue growth **20% YoY**.
- EBITDA margin **28.9%**.
- Q4 EBITDA margin **31.9%**.
- Gross margin above **50%**.
- PAT **₹391 crore**.
- Street expects **45% PAT CAGR**.

**Technology & Opportunities**
- Developing proprietary CNC controllers.
- In-house servo drives and motors.
- AI and nano-precision capabilities.
- Semiconductor equipment opportunity.
- Import substitution potential.

**Industry Tailwinds**
- EMS demand rising sharply.
- Defence capex accelerating.
- CNC replacement cycle beginning.
- India imports ~60% CNC machines.
- Domestic manufacturing gaining share.

**French Probe**
- Probe limited to Huron subsidiary.
- Revenue impact was deferred.
- No order cancellations reported.
- India operations unaffected.
- Management sees normal operations.

**Key Risks**
- Revenue missed guidance.
- Working capital remains elevated.
- Huron impacted consolidated PAT.
- French probe uncertainty persists.
- Promoter pledge at **15.4%**.
- No dividend payout.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Despite a sharp correction, Jyoti CNC retains strong long-term growth drivers through a record order book, aggressive capacity expansion, localisation of critical technologies and favourable industry tailwinds. However, investors should closely monitor execution, working capital, Huron-related developments and delivery of management's growth guidance.**

reports.text: SBI Securities Pick of the Day- Himadri Speciality Chemical Ltd., with | TP 800 |

**Kusumgar | Business & Valuation View**

**Business Profile**
- Specialized technical fabrics manufacturer.
- Around **50%** revenue from aerospace & defence.
- Operates in a niche segment.
- No listed domestic peer.

**Brokerage View**
- Emkay has initiated coverage.
- Target price: **₹800**.
- Valuation benchmarked to aerospace & defence peers.

**Valuation Perspective**
- Business remains primarily a textile manufacturer.
- Aerospace exposure enhances growth prospects.
- Premium valuation driven by niche positioning.
- Current valuation leaves limited margin of safety.

**Investment Considerations**
- IPO price of **₹419** offered reasonable valuation.
- Current price around **₹580** appears more demanding.
- Small-cap bull market could support premium multiples.
- Lack of listed peers may sustain valuation premium.

**Key Risks**
- Elevated valuation risk.
- Multiple contraction if sentiment weakens.
- Execution of growth plans remains critical.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kusumgar offers a unique niche play in specialized technical fabrics with significant exposure to the aerospace & defence sector. While its differentiated business may justify a valuation premium, the sharp post-listing rally has reduced the margin of safety, making future returns increasingly dependent on sustained earnings execution rather than further valuation expansion.**

**@Stockupdate9**

**Jyoti CNC

One more report with 60% upside**

**Unimech Aerospace | Business Update**

**Core Business**
- Derives ~90% of revenue from aerospace tooling, supplying critical tools for Airbus, Boeing, Rolls-Royce, Pratt & Whitney and LEAP engine programs.
- 89% of revenue comes from exports.

**Diversification Push**
- Expanding beyond aerospace into nuclear, defence, semiconductors, energy and oil & gas through organic growth and acquisitions.

**Key Developments**
- Order book surged from ₹93 Cr to ₹314 Cr between Mar'25 and May'26.
- Won a ₹72 Cr NPCIL order for critical equipment in India's nuclear sector.
- Formed a 51:49 JV with Kanoo Group in Saudi Arabia to tap oil & gas, utilities and mining opportunities.
- Acquired Hobel Bellows for ₹450 Cr, adding metallic bellows, tubing and precision assemblies to its portfolio.
- Increased stake in Dheya Engineering to 30%, gaining exposure to micro gas turbines and defence applications.

**Operations**
- Operates through 3 manufacturing facilities with 2.73 lakh sq ft of manufacturing space.
- 5,500+ SKUs across 35 customers in 7 countries.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Unimech Aerospace is leveraging its precision engineering expertise to build a multi-sector presence across high-entry-barrier industries, transforming from a pure-play aerospace tooling company into a diversified precision engineering powerhouse with significantly larger addressable opportunities.**

**Stock insights in one page
****@Stockinfo333**

Brokerage note on DIVIS

Merck's Enlicitide scores FDA approval, +ve for Divi's

- Merck's enlicitide has received USFDA approval.  

- This is a high efficacy, oral cholesterol lowering agent (60% decrease from baseline in LDL-C). We expect this drug likely to see high volumes in developed markets.

- Since we think Divi's is likely to emerge as a supplier, this is a major positive for Divi's Lab. 

- As per our channel checks, one of the dedicated units is likely to be for this molecule.

**Newgen Software | Jefferies Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹630**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue met estimates.
- Profit exceeded expectations.
- Healthy operational performance.

**Business Performance**
- License revenue growth accelerated.
- SaaS revenue remained strong.
- US market drove growth.
- Core markets stayed weak.

**Growth Outlook**
- Healthy order bookings continued.
- Deal sizes expanded further.
- Growth expected to accelerate from FY27.
- FY26–FY29 revenue CAGR estimated at **10%**.
- FY26–FY29 EPS CAGR estimated at **12%**.

**Valuation**
- Trades at **20x PE**.
- Jefferies sees attractive risk-reward.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Newgen Software, expecting stronger growth supported by expanding deal sizes, healthy order inflows and improving license/SaaS momentum, while considering current valuations attractive for long-term investors.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

reports.text:
**360 One WAM | Jefferies Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹1,340**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Profit at **₹330 crore**.
- Profit grew **15% YoY**.
- Earnings in line with estimates.

**Business Performance**
- Net inflows reached **₹10,800 crore**.
- Net flows at **3% of operating AUM**.
- Wealth inflows remained strong.
- AMC mandate outflows offset.

**Growth Outlook**
- HNI segment scaling up.
- Mass affluent expansion continues.
- Profit growth expected from FY28E.
- FY26–FY29 EPS CAGR estimated at **17%**.

**Management Commentary**
- CEO reaffirmed long-term commitment.
- Leadership continuity concerns eased.

**Valuation**
- Trades at **33x FY27E PE**.
- Jefferies sees valuations as attractive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains constructive on 360 One WAM, citing strong net inflows, expanding HNI and mass affluent franchises, improving earnings outlook and management stability, while viewing current valuations as attractive for long-term growth.**

**Piramal Finance | Jefferies Maintains Hold**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Hold** rating.
- Target price **₹2,300**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- PAT at **₹460 crore**.
- PAT slightly above estimates.
- Lower tax boosted earnings.
- Higher associate income supported PAT.
- PBT missed estimates.
- Higher wholesale provisions impacted PBT.

**Business Performance**
- Consolidated AUM grew **25% YoY**.
- NIM improved sequentially.
- Retail GNPA edged up QoQ.

**Outlook**
- Healthy AUM growth expected.
- Lower funding costs to aid NIM.
- Operating leverage to improve profits.
- RoA expected to expand over FY26–FY29.

**Valuation**
- Trades at **1.6x FY27E BV**.
- FY28/FY29 ROE seen at **10%****/12****%**.
- Upside appears limited.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies expects Piramal Finance to deliver healthy profit growth through strong AUM expansion, improving margins and operating leverage, but believes the current valuation adequately reflects these positives, maintaining a Hold rating.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Piramal Finance | Nomura Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹2,550**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Net profit beat estimates by **14%**.
- Better-than-expected earnings performance.

**Asset Quality**
- Improvement in unsecured portfolio.
- Digital loan quality strengthened.
- Mortgage portfolio remained stable.

**Capital & Growth**
- Announced **₹4,000 crore** fund raise.
- Strengthens growth capital.
- Supports future loan expansion.

**Outlook**
- On track for **2.5% ROAUM** by **Q4FY27E**.
- Profitability expected to improve further.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on Piramal Finance, highlighting stronger-than-expected earnings, improving asset quality in unsecured and digital lending, and the planned capital raise, which together support its profitability and growth outlook.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Wipro | Jefferies Maintains Underperform**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Underperform** rating.
- Target price **₹150**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Margins declined **140 bps QoQ**.
- Revenue guidance disappointed.
- Sep-26 CC growth guided at **-1.5% to +0.5% QoQ**.
- Key negative surprise for the quarter.

**Growth Outlook**
- Organic revenue growth remains weak.
- Three years of muted growth.
- FY27 recovery seen as unlikely.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27–FY29 revenue cut **2–5%**.
- FY27–FY29 PAT cut **2–5%**.
- FY27–FY29 EPS CAGR estimated at **5%**.

**Valuation**
- Dividend yield around **5%**.
- Risk-reward remains unattractive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains cautious on Wipro, citing weak organic growth, disappointing near-term guidance and slower earnings outlook. Despite an attractive dividend yield, it believes limited earnings growth makes the stock unattractive, maintaining an Underperform rating.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Wipro | Nomura Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹190**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Quarter remained subdued.
- Performance broadly in line.
- Deal wins were weak.

**Outlook**
- Q2FY27 guidance slightly below estimates.
- Near-term growth remains moderate.
- Recovery expected over time.

**Margin Outlook**
- Target EBIT margin **17–17.5%**.
- Margin improvement expected gradually.

**Valuation & Dividend**
- FY27E dividend yield around **5%**.
- Dividend expected to support valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on Wipro despite a soft Q1 and weaker deal wins, expecting gradual margin recovery and attractive dividend yield to support the stock while maintaining a Buy rating.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Tech Mahindra | Jefferies Maintains Underperform**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Underperform** rating.
- Target price **₹1,260**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue exceeded estimates.
- Margins beat expectations.
- Profit matched estimates.
- Strong operational execution.

**Growth Outlook**
- Robust deal wins continue.
- FY27 CC revenue growth seen at **6%**.
- Strong demand pipeline supports growth.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised **6–8%**.
- Reflects Q1 earnings beat.
- CC revenue CAGR estimated at **4%**.
- EPS CAGR estimated at **9%**.

**Valuation**
- Stock trades around **20x PE**.
- Valuations considered expensive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies acknowledges Tech Mahindra's improving execution, healthy deal wins and stronger earnings outlook, but believes the current valuation already reflects these positives, leading it to maintain an Underperform rating.**

**Tech Mahindra | Nomura Maintains Neutral**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price **₹1,600**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Beat estimates across parameters.
- Strong operational execution.
- Performance exceeded expectations.

**Outlook**
- On track to achieve FY27 goals.
- Growth momentum remains strong.
- Expected to outperform large-cap peers.
- Strong FY27–FY28 growth outlook.

**Valuation**
- Target multiple raised to **18x**.
- Earlier target multiple **16x**.
- Stock trades at **17.1x FY28E EPS**.
- FY28E EPS estimated at **₹88.5**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura acknowledges Tech Mahindra's strong execution and improving growth outlook, raising its valuation multiple as the company is expected to outperform large-cap IT peers, while retaining a Neutral rating due to limited upside at current valuations.**

**Tech Mahindra | HSBC Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹1,635**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Results exceeded expectations.
- Revenue growth accelerated to **6% YoY**.
- Strong operational performance.

**Margin Outlook**
- EBIT margin expansion on track.
- Q2FY27 margin guided at **14.4%**.
- FY27 EBIT margin target **15%**.

**Growth Outlook**
- Positive Q2FY27 outlook.
- Confident FY27 growth guidance.
- Better execution than peers.

**Valuation View**
- Superior growth supports premium valuation.
- HSBC sees valuation premium as justified.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains positive on Tech Mahindra, citing accelerating revenue growth, continued margin expansion and stronger execution versus peers, which together support its premium valuation and Buy rating.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Polycab India | HSBC Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹10,160**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Earnings beat estimates by **~10%**.
- Cables & Wires growth driven by realizations.
- Strong FMEG performance supported earnings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy domestic demand.
- Geographic expansion underway.
- FMEG business scaling rapidly.
- Structural growth outlook remains strong.

**Outlook**
- FY26–FY29 EPS CAGR estimated at **21%**.
- Well positioned for long-term growth.
- Supported by diversified product portfolio.

**Valuation**
- Trading at **35x FY28E PE**.

**Key Risks**
- Commodity price volatility.
- Copper price fluctuations.
- Aluminium price fluctuations.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains constructive on Polycab, expecting strong earnings growth driven by robust demand, expanding market presence and improving FMEG profitability, while highlighting commodity price volatility as the key downside risk.**

**Live Quarterly Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**Polycab India | Jefferies Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹11,100**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- C&W sales grew **39% YoY**.
- FMEG sales grew **71% YoY**.
- Growth achieved despite Middle East disruption.
- C&W growth largely price-led.

**Margins**
- C&W EBIT margin at **13.3%**.
- Margin declined **140 bps YoY**.
- FMEG EBIT margin improved to **8.0%**.
- FMEG margin expanded **590 bps YoY**.

**Growth Outlook**
- Strong power sector opportunity.
- Capex cycle remains supportive.
- FY26–FY29 EPS CAGR estimated at **22%**.

**Earnings & Valuation**
- FY27–FY28 EPS raised **2–3%**.
- Stock gained **21%** in one month.
- Trading at **42x FY27E PE**.
- Trading at **35x FY28E PE**.
- Above **37x** five-year average.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Polycab, citing strong execution, robust growth across Cables & Wires and FMEG, and continued benefits from India's power and capex cycle, despite rich valuations following the recent rally.**

**ABB India | Macquarie Maintains Underperform**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Underperform** rating.
- Target price **₹5,470**.

**Parent Q2CY26 Highlights**
- India orders increased **81% YoY**.
- Strong order booking momentum.
- Revenue outlook remains healthy.

**India Outlook**
- Expects **15–20%** revenue growth.
- Driven by robust execution.
- Positive demand environment continues.

**Key Concerns**
- West Asia conflict impacted profitability.
- Slower project execution.
- Unfavourable revenue mix.
- Elevated commodity costs.
- Foreign exchange volatility.

**Brokerage View**
- Margin pressures may persist.
- Near-term profitability likely constrained.
- Execution remains key monitorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- **While Macquarie acknowledges strong order momentum and healthy revenue growth prospects for ABB India, it remains cautious on profitability due to execution challenges, cost inflation, adverse business mix and geopolitical headwinds, maintaining an Underperform rating.**

**ABB India | UBS Reiterates Buy**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Buy** rating.
- Target price **₹8,030**.

**Key Highlights**
- Parent results indicate stronger India orders.
- Order momentum accelerating sharply.
- Positive outlook for India business.

**Growth Drivers**
- Large marine project wins.
- Strong data centre demand.
- Healthy rail sector orders.
- Double-digit pricing benefits.
- Improvement in base orders.

**Parent Commentary**
- Positive outlook on India reiterated.
- Plans **US$75 million** investment.
- Expanding manufacturing capacity.
- Strengthening R&D capabilities.
- India to serve regional markets.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS remains positive on ABB India, citing accelerating order momentum, strong demand across high-growth sectors and increased parent investment, which should strengthen manufacturing, innovation and long-term growth prospects.**

**BHEL | UBS Maintains Neutral**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price **₹460**.

**Q1FY27 Performance**
- Revenue grew **40% YoY**.
- EBITDA at **₹500 crore**.
- Revenue beat UBS estimates by **20%**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **51%**.
- EBITDA margin expanded to **6.5%**.
- Margin exceeded **5.2%** estimate.

**Order Momentum**
- New orders **₹26,700 crore**.
- Driven by large power orders.
- Strong order pipeline maintained.

**Power Business**
- Revenue grew **52% YoY**.
- PBIT margin improved to **9.5%**.
- Execution remained robust.

**Industry Business**
- Orders declined **70% YoY**.
- Revenue increased **12% YoY**.
- PBIT margin moderated to **13.6%**.
- Weak business mix impacted margins.

**Export & Margins**
- Won largest-ever **₹2,300 crore** GTG export order.
- Gross margin expanded **200 bps YoY**.
- Gross margin reached **31.2%**.
- Sequential decline due to mix.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS views BHEL's Q1FY27 positively, supported by strong execution, better-than-expected profitability, robust power segment performance and healthy order inflows, while maintaining a Neutral rating as sustained execution and margin delivery remain key to future earnings growth.**

**BHEL | Macquarie Raises Target Price**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price raised to **₹315**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue increased **40% YoY**.
- Strong execution momentum.
- EBITDA margin improved to **6.5%**.
- PAT reached **₹380 crore**.

**Order Book**
- New orders at **₹26,740 crore**.
- Orders doubled versus Q1FY26.
- Order backlog at **₹2,60,300 crore**.
- Backlog increased **27% YoY**.
- Healthy revenue visibility ahead.

**Brokerage Concerns**
- Sustained execution remains critical.
- Profitability improvement still challenging.
- High receivables continue to be a key risk.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie acknowledges BHEL's strong Q1FY27 execution, robust order inflows and expanding backlog, but remains Neutral as sustained execution, margin improvement and reduction in receivables are essential for a meaningful re-rating.**

**BHEL | JPMorgan Maintains Underperform**

**Brokerage View**
- Rating maintained **Underperform**.
- Target price **₹220**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Improved execution performance.
- Margin expansion continued.
- Better operating performance.

**Brokerage Concerns**
- Stock up **71%** in one year.
- Up **41%** in last three months.
- Significant outperformance versus Nifty.
- Valuations seen as stretched.

**Order Outlook**
- Thermal ordering peak likely over.
- FY26 total orders declined **19% YoY**.
- Power orders declined **27% YoY**.
- FY27 order inflows may fall **12% YoY**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **While BHEL delivered better execution and margin performance in Q1FY27, JPMorgan believes the market has already priced in the thermal power upcycle and sees limited upside amid slowing order inflows, maintaining an Underperform rating with a ₹220 target price.**
